audit_items.proof_trace: damon_sysfs_target_release makes zero allocations of its own and has no error paths; it is a kobject release callback that correctly frees only the damon_sysfs_target struct via kfree. The sole sub-resource, target->regions, is a child kobject registered with parent=&target->kobj via kobject_init_and_add, meaning the kobject framework's parent-reference invariant (kobject_add_internal calls kobject_get(parent)) guarantees damon_sysfs_regions_release fires and frees regions before this callback can ever execute. On all error paths in damon_sysfs_targets_add_dirs, target->regions is NULL when kobject_put(&target->kobj) is called (damon_sysfs_target_add_dirs only assigns target->regions on success), so kfree(target) is always safe.
